name: get_websites
description: Get list of all websites configured in Umami. Returns website ID, name, domain, and createdAt timestamp. CRITICAL: Always call this FIRST before any analytics queries to (1) verify the website exists, (2) check when it was created, and (3) ensure you don't request data from before the creation date. Analytics data only exists from createdAt onwards. If UMAMI_TEAM_ID is configured, websites are fetched from that team automatically.

includeTeams.description: Set to true to include websites where you are the team owner. Useful when websites are shared across teams and not visible by default.

name: get_stats
description: Get aggregated statistics for a website. Returns flat numeric fields: pageviews, visitors (unique sessions), visits, bounces, and totaltime. May include a 'comparison' object with the same fields representing the previous period of the same length. IMPORTANT: First check website createdAt date. If requesting 'last X days', verify that X days ago is after createdAt - if not, adjust start_date to createdAt. Note: 'visitors' = unique sessions, 'bounces' = single-pageview sessions, 'totaltime' = sum of time between pageviews (excludes bounces).

start_date.description: Start date. Accepts ISO 8601 date strings (e.g. '2026-03-23' or '2026-03-23T00:00:00Z') or Unix timestamps in milliseconds (13 digits). ISO dates are RECOMMENDED to avoid calculation errors. MUST be after website createdAt.

end_date.description: End date. Accepts ISO 8601 date strings (e.g. '2026-03-23' or '2026-03-23T23:59:59Z') or Unix timestamps in milliseconds. ISO dates are RECOMMENDED. Must be after start_date.

name: get_pageviews
description: Get pageview and session data grouped by time unit. Returns 'pageviews' array (total views) and 'sessions' array (unique visitors) per time period. Time labels in the 't' field correspond to the unit parameter. Always verify the website was created before your start_date, otherwise you'll get empty or misleading results.

start_date.description: Start date. Accepts ISO 8601 date strings (e.g. '2026-03-23') or Unix timestamps in milliseconds. ISO dates are RECOMMENDED. MUST be after website createdAt.

end_date.description: End date. Accepts ISO 8601 date strings (e.g. '2026-03-23') or Unix timestamps in milliseconds. ISO dates are RECOMMENDED. Must be after start_date.

unit.description: Time unit for grouping data. Determines granularity and number of data points returned

name: get_metrics
description: Get metrics for a website. Returns array with 'x' (metric value) and 'y' (count). For 'url'/'path': page paths without query params. For 'referrer': traffic sources (empty string = direct). For 'browser/os/device': user agents. For 'country': 2-letter ISO codes. IMPORTANT: Check website createdAt first - requesting data before creation returns empty results.

start_date.description: Start date. Accepts ISO 8601 date strings (e.g. '2026-03-23') or Unix timestamps in milliseconds. ISO dates are RECOMMENDED. MUST be after website createdAt.

end_date.description: End date. Accepts ISO 8601 date strings (e.g. '2026-03-23') or Unix timestamps in milliseconds. ISO dates are RECOMMENDED. Must be after start_date.

metric_type.description: Type of metric to retrieve. 'url' is accepted as an alias for 'path'. Each returns different data in 'x' field

limit.description: Maximum results to return. Use higher values (50-100) for complete data. Default may miss important items

name: get_active
description: Get count of current active visitors on the website in real-time. Returns array with 'x' field containing the visitor count as a string. No date parameters needed as this shows current state only.

name: list_reports
description: List saved Umami dashboard reports for a website. Saved reports cover Umami creations such as goals, funnels, attribution, breakdowns, journeys, retention/cohort-style reports, revenue, UTM, performance, and insights depending on your Umami version.

name: get_report
description: Get a saved Umami report by ID, including its report type and parameters.

report_id.description: Saved report ID from list_reports or create_report

name: create_report
description: Create a saved Umami dashboard report. Use type 'goal' for goals, 'funnel' for funnels, 'retention' for cohort-style retention reports, and the other supported types for Umami report creations. Segments and cohorts are referenced by UUID inside parameters or filters when supported by your Umami instance; Umami does not expose public segment/cohort CRUD endpoints.

parameters.description: Report-specific parameters. Examples: goal {"type":"event","value":"signup"}; funnel {"window":60,"steps":[{"type":"path","value":"/"},{"type":"event","value":"signup"}]}. Funnel step type MUST be "path" or "event"; this server normalizes common URL aliases to "path". May include segment/cohort UUID filters when supported by Umami.

name: update_report
description: Update a saved Umami dashboard report. Umami expects the full report definition, so include websiteId, type, name, and parameters.

parameters.description: Updated report-specific parameters. For funnel reports, steps must use type "path" or "event".

name: delete_report
description: Delete a saved Umami dashboard report by ID.

name: run_report
description: Run an Umami report query without saving it. Supports report endpoints such as attribution, breakdown, funnel, goal/goals, journey, performance, retention, revenue, UTM, and insights depending on your Umami version. Use payload for the Umami request body, including websiteId, date range, timezone, parameters, filters, segment UUID, or cohort UUID.

payload.description: Request body sent directly to Umami's /api/reports/{report_type} endpoint. For funnel reports, steps must use type "path" or "event"; URL aliases are normalized to "path".
